uplink.description: weather report (0B 2 00): temp/humidity/pressure + wind + rain + solar/uv extras
uplink._note: Synthetic in-bounds weather frame. Bytes hand-built to the upstream decoder-weather.js parseWeather layout (Davis Vantage Pro2: pressure 0.001 inHg, temp 0.1 degF, wind mph, rain 0.2 mm counts); expected.data produced by running reference/upstream-codec.js (ORACLE) and remapping its keys to the normalized vocabulary.

uplink.description: weather report (0B 2 00): wind direction 360 folds to 0 (vocab requires <360)
uplink._note: Synthetic. Upstream reports wind direction 360 (due north); our codec folds 360 to 0 to satisfy the vocabulary bound 0 <= direction < 360. Every other value matches the upstream ORACLE run of reference/upstream-codec.js.

uplink.description: PM report (0B 2 01): particulate matter pm1/pm2.5/pm10 with timestamp
uplink._note: Synthetic PM frame. pm1/pm25/pm10 values produced by the upstream ORACLE (reference/upstream-codec.js parsePM). Upstream emits the timestamp as a non-deterministic locale string; we emit RFC3339 UTC (decoded from the same packed 4-byte date), a deliberate fork for the `time` vocabulary key.

uplink.description: TER+PM report (0B 3 00) with battery: temp/humidity/pressure + PM + batteryPercent
uplink._note: Synthetic TER+PM frame (MCF integer-hundredths temp/pressure, half-percent humidity). Numeric values produced by the upstream ORACLE (reference/upstream-codec.js parseTERPM); battery is a PERCENTAGE so it is emitted as batteryPercent (vocabulary `battery` is volts), and the date is re-emitted as RFC3339 UTC.

uplink.description: TER+PM report (0B 3 00) without trailing battery byte; negative temperature
uplink._note: Synthetic TER+PM frame with no battery byte (length 19) and a negative temperature (MCF signed 16-bit). Values produced by the upstream ORACLE (reference/upstream-codec.js parseTERPM).

Synthetic error case. Uplink id 0x01 (time sync) is a non-measurement housekeeping frame; this normalized codec only decodes the 0x0B report-data weather frames and rejects others rather than emitting a bare {}.
